item.title: The dollar swap puzzle
item.description: Plus, where’s the love for clean energy?

item.title: Strategy’s teetering financial tower
item.description: A bitcoin price reprieve can’t erase the company’s contradictions

item.title: Banks charged sharply different fees for access to Anthropic investment
item.description: Disparate pricing raises prospect of investors earning unequal returns in the same deal on the basis of who their banker is

item.title: How Swiss concessions failed to ease UBS’s $20bn capital concerns
item.description: Switzerland’s Federal Council this week laid out a compromise which set up another fight with the country’s biggest bank

item.title: JD Sports chair quit after failing to convince board to oust CEO
item.description: Andy Higginson stepped down this week after pushing for CEO Régis Schultz to be replaced

item.title: Europe’s M&A dreams face a German political test
item.description: Large mergers in Germany could create ‘European champions’ — but will Berlin stand aside?

item.title: Rightwing media mogul rattles France’s literary scene
item.description: Writers flee Paris publishing house Grasset in protest at conservative owner Vincent Bolloré

item.title: Consumers turn to AI for investment decisions
item.description: Gen Z and millennials the most likely groups to consult chatbots on money matters

item.title: Chinese bid for UK chemical plant condemned as threat to competition
item.description: World’s largest producer of titanium dioxide to acquire facility in North East of England

item.title: Mercuria to invest in smelters and mines as it expands metals push
item.description: Swiss trading house’s move to take 25% stake in aluminium plant in Indonesia marks shift into asset ownership

item.title: Super League architect Andrea Agnelli makes comeback as sports investor
item.description: Italian executive launches investment firm five years after his failed attempt to reconfigure European football

item.title: Swatch’s governance faces further challenge
item.description: Fresh push by shareholder activist raises debate over board

item.title: America must guard against China’s own Mythos
item.description: The US needs to block Chinese access to the most advanced technology

item.title: Morgan McSweeney held talks with Google DeepMind over AI project
item.description: Former Labour chief of staff pitched venture to tech group about the crossover between artificial intelligence and democratic politics

item.title: KPMG and EY demote partners in end of job-for-life model
item.description: Move is a departure from the practice of simply asking underperforming senior partners to retire

item.title: How Long Gone’s Chris Black talks taste
item.description: The podcast co-host and Hanover founder loves his Contax T3 and ‘a freaky amount’ of caffeine

item.title: Singapore turns to Russian fuel oil as war restricts Middle Eastern supplies
item.description: Imports from Russia to world’s biggest ship refuelling port surge in March and April

item.title: Donald Trump and the remaking of America’s media order
item.description: The president once railed against the ‘fake news’ from the outside. Today, the system itself bears his imprint

item.title: MPS chief considering Generali stake sale
item.description: The proceeds would help fund tie-up between Monte dei Paschi di Siena and Banco BPM

item.title: Trump suggests US could buy Spirit amid bailout talks
item.description: Bankrupt low-cost airline has been in talks with the federal government for a ‘financing package’

item.title: Middle East crisis day 55 as it happened: Donald Trump says Israel and Lebanon will extend ceasefire by 3 weeks
item.description: President says American navy will ‘shoot and kill’ any boat laying mines in Strait of Hormuz

item.title: FirstFT: US accuses China of ‘industrial-scale’ theft of AI technology
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: PwC fined $166mn by Hong Kong regulators, and Meta to cut 10% of jobs

item.title: Griffin’s Citadel attacks Mamdani for using his penthouse to sell New York tax plan
item.description: Mayor called out billionaire’s $238mn property as he pushes for second-home levy on the wealthy

item.title: AI brings Foxconn a chance to cut its reliance on Apple
item.description: The cloud and networking division, which assembles AI servers, is growing at a pace the smartphone market cannot match

item.title: US shale bosses resist oil output boost amid Iran war ‘chaos’
item.description: Dallas Fed survey finds that energy executives are not confident high prices will hold

item.title: UK in talks with Anthropic over Mythos access for banks
item.description: British lenders seek advice from US groups testing powerful new cyber security AI model

item.title: Intel shares jump 15% as it predicts revenue surge from AI data centres
item.description: US chipmaker reports better than expected revenue and financial guidance

item.title: Vanguard chief warns of ‘financial exploitation’ by prediction markets
item.description: Boss of $12tn asset manager echoes criticism of platforms such as Polymarket and Kalshi

item.title: Meta to cut 10% of jobs to ‘offset’ Zuckerberg’s AI spending
item.description: Social media group plans to spend $135bn on data centres this year

item.title: China hackers steal western secrets by targeting consumer gadgets
item.description: Compromised home internet routers and smart fridges are being used in cyber attacks, intelligence agencies warn
